## 1. Regulatory Deep Dive

### Overview

Forex.com holds 5 regulatory licenses including the extremely rare CFTC/NFA registration for US clients. This makes it one of the most heavily regulated forex brokers globally, with Tier 1 regulation in the US, UK, and Australia.

### License Portfolio

#### License #1: Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) / National Futures Association (NFA) — United States

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Regulator** | CFTC / NFA |
| **License Number** | NFA ID 0339826 |
| **Verification Link** | [NFA BASIC](https://www.nfa.futures.org/basicnet/) |
| **Entity Name** | FOREX.com (GAIN Capital) |
| **Status** | ✅ Active — Registered |
| **Jurisdiction** | United States |
| **License Type** | Retail Foreign Exchange Dealer (RFED) |
| **Date Obtained** | 2001 |

**What This Means For You:**

- **Leverage Limits:** Up to 1:50 for major pairs, 1:20 for minors (CFTC regulations — among the strictest globally)
- **Deposit Protection:** NFA membership; CFTC-regulated segregation requirements; no explicit deposit insurance but strict capital requirements
- **Negative Balance Protection:** Yes — CFTC requires this for all US retail forex
- **Complaint Process:** NFA Arbitration → CFTC reclamation fund if NFA fails
- **Segregation of Funds:** Yes — strict CFTC requirements; daily segregation reporting

**Countries Served Under This License:**
- ✅ United States (US persons — the only broker in this analysis that can legally serve US retail forex clients)
- ❌ Non-US clients directed to other entities

**Regulatory History:**

| Date | Event | Details |
|------|-------|---------|
| 2001 | NFA Registration | Became registered RFED with NFA |
| 2010 | Enhanced Compliance | Adapted to Dodd-Frank Act requirements |
| 2019 | Ownership Change | GAIN Capital acquired; NFA registration maintained |
| 2022 | StoneX Acquisition | NFA registration transferred to StoneX Group |

> **Key Note:** US CFTC/NFA regulation is the strictest retail forex regulation in the world. Only a handful of brokers are registered. This provides the highest level of regulatory protection available.

- Access full TradingView charting tools
- Use TradingView's social features with real market data
- Execute orders without leaving TradingView

## 7. FAQ Section

### General

**Q: Is Forex.com a legitimate broker?**
A: Yes, Forex.com is one of the most legitimate and trusted forex brokers globally. It is owned by StoneX Group (NASDAQ: SNEX), a Fortune 100 company, and holds 5 regulatory licenses including the extremely rare CFTC/NFA registration for US clients. It has been operating since 1999.

**Q: What is the minimum deposit for Forex.com?**
A: The minimum deposit is $100 for Standard and Commission accounts, and $25,000 for DMA (Direct Market Access) accounts.

**Q: Is Forex.com regulated?**
A: Yes, Forex.com holds 5 regulatory licenses: CFTC/NFA (USA, 0339826), FCA (UK, 113942), ASIC (Australia, 401379), CySEC (Cyprus, 093/08), and CIRO (Canada, 249886). This is among the strongest regulatory profiles of any broker.

### Trading

**Q: What platforms does Forex.com offer?**
A: Forex.com offers its proprietary Advanced Trading Platform, MetaTrader 4, and native TradingView integration. All platforms are available on desktop, web, and mobile.

**Q: What is the maximum leverage on Forex.com?**
A: Maximum leverage is 1:50 for major forex pairs (US), 1:20 for minor pairs (US). UK/EU clients have up to 1:30 for retail, up to 1:200 for professional clients.

**Q: Does Forex.com allow scalping/EAs/news trading?**
A: Yes, Forex.com allows scalping, EAs, and news trading. The DMA account is specifically designed for these strategies with direct market access.

### Fees & Funding

**Q: What are the spreads on Forex.com?**
A: Standard account spreads start from 1.0 pips on EUR/USD. Commission account offers raw spreads from 0.0 pips with approximately $5 per round turn lot. Both are very competitive.

**Q: How long do withdrawals take?**
A: ACH withdrawals are processed within 1-2 business days. Bank wire withdrawals take 2-5 business days. Credit/debit card refunds take 3-7 business days.

**Q: Does Forex.com charge inactivity fees?**
A: Yes, Forex.com charges a $15 monthly inactivity fee after 12 months of no trading activity.

### Safety

**Q: Are my funds safe with Forex.com?**
A: Yes, Forex.com provides exceptional fund safety. Backed by StoneX Group (Fortune 100, $9.3B equity), regulated by 5 top-tier authorities, with strict CFTC/FCA segregation requirements and negative balance protection. This is one of the safest brokers available.
